title: monitoring — subprocessors
description: The Monitoring module engages SIX third-party subprocessors — one per provider driver. Every subprocessor receives system-health signals + alert metadata via HTTPS. NO customer PII flows through monitoring providers — the signals contain tenant_id + severity + timestamp + affected service + error messages. GDPR Art. 28 DPA references + Art. 30 records of processing activities. Every tenant that activates a provider effectively adds that subprocessor to their processing register; the tenant admin acknowledges the DPA reference before configuring.

subprocessors.purpose: Server-side error tracking + release health monitoring. Receives error events + performance signals from tenant infrastructure.
subprocessors.data_hashing: user_context + tenant_id hashed before dispatch. No raw customer PII.
subprocessors.activation_gate: tenant admin acknowledges the DPA before saving the provider config

subprocessors.purpose: APM + logs + metrics + events observability platform. Receives monitoring alerts + incident timelines correlated with APM traces + log lines.
subprocessors.data_hashing: tenant_id hashed before dispatch; no raw customer PII
subprocessors.activation_gate: tenant admin acknowledges DPA + selects the deployment region

subprocessors.purpose: Digital operations platform. Receives P1 + P2 alerts + routes them to the tenant's on-call rotation via SMS, phone, push, and email.
subprocessors.data_hashing: tenant_id + alert_id + subject identifiers hashed before dispatch

subprocessors.purpose: On-call management + alerting platform (Atlassian). Alternative + fallback to PagerDuty.
subprocessors.activation_gate: tenant admin acknowledges DPA + selects region

subprocessors.purpose: Tenant-hosted Prometheus + AlertManager. Stackra pushes metrics to the tenant's Pushgateway + optionally acts as an AlertManager webhook receiver (accepts POST from tenant Prometheus + re-emits as internal alerts).
data_categories: prometheus text-format metrics (tenant_hash + severity + service labels)
subprocessors.dpa_url: N/A — the tenant is responsible for their own infrastructure
subprocessors.activation_gate: tenant admin confirms pushgateway URL + acknowledges own DPA responsibility

subprocessors.purpose: Generic HMAC-signed JSON webhook. Escape hatch for tenants using Grafana / Grafana Cloud / New Relic / Slack directly / etc. Data ultimately delivered to a caller-configured URL.
data_categories: full alert / incident / health check JSON (tenant_hash + severity + affected services + timestamps)
subprocessors.data_hashing: tenant_id hashed; no raw customer PII
subprocessors.dpa_url: N/A — the tenant is responsible for their downstream endpoint's DPA
subprocessors.activation_gate: tenant admin confirms URL + acknowledges own DPA responsibility

downstream_subprocessors_referenced.used_for: Dispatching MonitoringAlertFiredNotification / MonitoringIncidentOpenedNotification / MonitoringIncidentEscalatedNotification / MonitoringPostmortemRequiredNotification / MonitoringHealthCheckPersistentlyFailingNotification / MonitoringProviderCircuitBreakerOpenedNotification / MonitoringWeeklyDigestNotification via mail / SMS / push / Slack channels.

downstream_subprocessors_referenced.reference: compliance::encryption owns the encryption service used for at-rest encryption of monitoring_provider_configs.config.

notes: This file exists deliberately with 6 active subprocessors. Every tenant that configures a monitoring provider effectively activates that subprocessor for their tenant.
The tenant-facing subprocessor list (compliance/subprocessors surface) reads from this file + shows only the active subset per tenant.
Adding a new provider driver = adding a new entry here + updating the tenant-facing subprocessor list + updating compliance.json Art. 28 evidence.

Unlike marketing subprocessors (which receive hashed customer PII for ad targeting), monitoring subprocessors receive ONLY system-health signals — tenant_id is the only correlation surface + it's hashed.
